Title: Successful User Login
Preconditions:
- User account exists in the system with valid credentials.
- System is operational.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login screen.
2. Enter `john_doe` in the username field.
3. Enter `SecurePass123` in the password field.
4. Click the "Login" button.
Expected Result:
- User is successfully logged in.
- System redirects to the product browsing page.
- `login()` method returns `Ret` (success status).

Title: Failed User Login (Invalid Password)
Preconditions: - User account exists with valid username but incorrect password.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login screen.
2. Enter `john_doe` in the username field.
3. Enter `WrongPass456` in the password field.
4. Click the "Login" button.
Expected Result:
- System displays an error message: "Invalid username or password."
- User remains on the login screen.
- `login()` method returns `Ret` (failure status).

Title: Successful Admin Login
Preconditions: - Admin account exists with valid credentials.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login screen.
2. Enter `admin_user` in the username field.
3. Enter `AdminPass789` in the password field.
4. Click the "Login" button.
Expected Result:
- Admin is successfully logged in.
- System redirects to the admin dashboard (product management page).
- `login()` method returns `Ret` (success status).

Title: User Logout
Test Steps:
1. Click the "Logout" button in the top-right corner.
2. Confirm logout in the confirmation dialog (if prompted).
Expected Result:
- User is logged out successfully.
- System redirects to the login screen.
- `logout()` method returns `Ret` (success status).
- User session is invalidated.

Title: User Views Product Details
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- At least one product exists in the system (e.g., "Laptop Pro").
Test Data:
- Product name: `Laptop Pro`
- Description: `High-performance laptop with 16GB RAM`
Test Steps:
1. Browse to the product listing page.
2. Click on "Laptop Pro."
3. Verify product details are displayed.
Expected Result:
- Product name, description, and price are displayed correctly.
- `view(username: String)` method executes without errors.
- User can see the "Add to Cart" button.

Title: Admin Updates Product Description
Preconditions:
- Admin is logged in.
- A product exists (e.g., "Smartphone X").
Test Data:
- Old description: `Latest smartphone with 5G support`
- New description: `Latest smartphone with 5G, improved battery life`
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the product management page.
2. Search for "Smartphone X."
3. Click "Edit" next to the product.
4. Update the description to: `Latest smartphone with 5G, improved battery life`.
5. Click "Save."
Expected Result:
- Product description is updated successfully.
- `update(username: String, description: String)` method returns `Ret` (success status).
- Updated description is reflected in the product listing.

Title: Admin Attempts to Update Product with Empty Description
Preconditions:
- Admin is logged in.
- A product exists.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the product management page.
2. Search for a product and click "Edit."
3. Leave the description field empty.
4. Click "Save."
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: "Description cannot be empty."
- Product description remains unchanged.
- `update()` method returns `Ret` (failure status).

Title: User Adds Product to Cart
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- At least one product exists (e.g., "Wireless Headphones").
Test Steps:
1. Browse to the product listing page.
2. Click "Add to Cart" for "Wireless Headphones."
3. Verify the cart icon updates to show 1 item.
Expected Result:
- Product is added to the cart.
- `add(product: Product)` method returns `Ret` (success status).
- Cart now contains "Wireless Headphones."

Title: User Removes Product from Cart
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- Cart contains at least one product (e.g., "Wireless Headphones").
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the cart page.
2. Click "Remove" next to "Wireless Headphones."
3. Confirm removal in the dialog.
Expected Result:
- Product is removed from the cart.
- `remove(product: Product)` method returns `Ret` (success status).
- Cart is now empty (or shows 0 items).

Title: User Attempts to Remove Non-Existent Product from Cart
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- Cart is empty.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the cart page.
2. Attempt to click "Remove" on a product not in the cart (e.g., manually trigger via dev tools).
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: "Product not found in cart."
- Cart remains empty.
- `remove()` method returns `Ret` (failure status).

Title: Successful User Checkout
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- Cart contains at least one product (e.g., "Smartwatch").
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the cart page.
2. Click "Proceed to Checkout."
3. Verify order summary matches cart contents.
4. Click "Confirm Checkout."
Expected Result:
- Checkout is successful.
- `checkout()` method returns `Ret` (success status).
- User sees an order confirmation page.
- Cart is emptied.

Title: Checkout with Empty Cart
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- Cart is empty.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the cart page.
2. Click "Proceed to Checkout."
Expected Result:
- System displays an error: "Your cart is empty. Add items to checkout."
- User is redirected to the product listing page.
- `checkout()` method returns `Ret` (failure status).

Title: Menu-Driven Navigation (Yes/No Selection Flow)
Preconditions:
- User is on the home screen.
- Menu options are visible (e.g., "Do you want to browse products?").
Test Steps:
1. Click the menu option to start navigation.
2. Select "Yes" when prompted: "Add item to cart?"
3. Select "No" when prompted: "Remove this item?"
4. Select "Note" (if applicable) for additional options.
5. Verify redirection to the home screen.
Expected Result:
- Each selection triggers the correct action:
  - "Yes" → Product is added to cart.
  - "No" → Cart remains unchanged.
  - "Note" → Additional options (if any) are displayed.
- Final step redirects to home screen ("Ritmo alla home").
- No errors occur during navigation.

Title: Invalid Menu Selection (Edge Case)
Preconditions: - User is on the menu screen.
Test Steps:
1. Click the menu option to start navigation.
2. Manually trigger an invalid selection (e.g., via dev tools to simulate a broken UI state).
Expected Result:
- System handles the error gracefully (e.g., resets to the menu or shows a default option).
- No crashes or unexpected behavior.
- User can retry the selection.

Title: End-to-End User Flow (Browse → Add to Cart → Checkout)
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- At least one product exists (e.g., "Bluetooth Speaker").
Test Steps:
1. Browse to the product listing page.
2. Click "Add to Cart" for "Bluetooth Speaker."
3. Verify cart updates (1 item).
4. Click "Proceed to Checkout."
5. Confirm checkout.
Expected Result:
- All steps complete successfully:
  - Product is added to cart (`add()`).
  - Cart is displayed correctly.
  - Checkout is processed (`checkout()`).
- User sees order confirmation.
- Cart is emptied post-checkout.

Title: Admin Product Update Affects User View
Preconditions:
- Admin is logged in.
- User is logged in (separate session).
- A product exists (e.g., "Tablet").
Test Steps:
1. Admin updates the product description for "Tablet" to: "New model with stylus support."
2. Log out as Admin and log in as User.
3. Browse to the product listing and click on "Tablet."
Expected Result:
- User sees the updated description: "New model with stylus support."
- Changes are reflected in real-time (no caching issues).
- `update()` and `view()` methods sync correctly.

Title: Concurrent Cart Modifications (Race Condition)
Preconditions:
- Two users are logged in simultaneously.
- Cart contains 1 item (e.g., "Backpack").
Test Steps:
1. User1 adds another item to the cart ("Headphones").
2. Simultaneously, User2 removes the "Backpack" from their cart.
3. User1 proceeds to checkout.
Expected Result:
- System handles concurrent modifications without data corruption.
- User1’s cart reflects only "Headphones."
- No duplicate or missing items in the cart.
- Checkout completes successfully for User1.
